Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x73FC "SKULL-9E80-73FC" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 lda #$36 sta $01 jsr $9e80 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Skull.prg"
Metadata.Notes:
Added an init routine at $9E80 which: 1. Turns off interrupts from the two CIA chips and acks any pending CIA interrupts. 2. Copies 256 bytes of data from $9ED0 down to $0000 so that the dump can start at $0400. This allows the game to run correctly. 3. Sets up the correct VICII settings and border colour. 4. Sets 2 bytes of return address data at the top of the stack ($01FE - $01FF). 5. Sets the stack pointer to $FD to give the correct next available location on the stack (i.e. just below the address data copied in step 4). 6. Re-enables raster interrupts and timer NMIs. 7. And finally jumps to the entry point at $73FC (it couldn't use RTS because of the stack manipulation above).
All of this allows the game to start more quickly.
